metadata.description: Comprehensive Edo-English dictionary for the BiZY Edo Translator app
metadata.author: BiZY Language Data

entries.example_english: How are you? I am fine.

entries.english: I am fine / It is good

entries.example_edo: Ẹghẹ ne ima khian, ọ da.
Ẹghẹ ne ọ rre, ima ya.
Orhue ọ dẹ vbe ẹvbo Ẹdo.
entries.example_english: White chalk is significant in Edo culture.
The king has come to the land.

entries.example_edo: Ẹko ẹre ọ rhie ẹmẹ uhunmwun.

entries.example_english: Palm oil comes from the palm tree.
Palm oil is in the soup.

entries.example_edo: Eba ne ẹmẹ uhunmwun ọ dẹ.
Ọmọ ẹrhan ọ dẹ ẹvbo Ẹdo.
entries.example_english: Palm kernels are common in Edo land.

entries.example_edo: Ẹtin ẹkhue ọ ye ẹmẹ uhunmwun.
entries.example_english: Bitter leaf is in the soup.

entries.example_edo: Ọsin ọ ye ẹmẹ uhunmwun ẹre.

entries.example_english: The monkey plays in the tree.

entries.example_edo: Ọ̃wan ne igbe ọmwan ọ rre.
Ẹẹ̃va ne igbe ọmwan ọ rre.
Ẹẹ̃han ne igbe ọmwan ọ rre.
Ẹẹ̃nẹ ne igbe ọmwan ọ rre.
Isiẹn ne igbe ọmwan ọ rre.

entries.example_english: He bought things from the market.
There is joy in his heart.
The leopard is in the bush.

entries.example_edo: Ọmwan ne ọ rẹn Ẹdo ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: One who knows Edo is blessed.

entries.example_edo: Ọmwan ne ọ ye ọ rre.

entries.example_english: The tree of the earth is tall.

entries.example_edo: Uke ẹmẹ ọ dẹ ẹvbo Ẹdo.
entries.example_english: Farming is important in Edo land.
The ancient king ruled the land.
The Edo royal ceremony has come.
He moved quickly along the road.
The matter is heavy on him.

entries.edo: Ẹtin ne ọ rre vbe agbon
entries.pronunciation: eh-tin neh oh reh vbeh ahg-bohn
entries.example_edo: Ẹtin ne ọ rre vbe agbon ọ dẹ.
Ọmwan ne ọ gbe ẹrhẹn ọ dẹ iran.

entries.example_english: All of us are going home.

entries.example_edo: Ima gha rẹn Ẹdo vbe iran.

entries.english: It is good in Edo land
entries.example_english: It is good in Edo land.

entries.example_edo: Ẹdẹ ẹre ọ ya vbe iran.
entries.example_english: The day has come for him.
He has love for his homeland.
The sound of music is here.

entries.edo: Ọ tua emwin ne ọ dẹ
entries.pronunciation: oh too-ah em-win neh oh deh
entries.example_edo: Ọ tua emwin ne ọ dẹ iran.
Ẹzọ ẹre ọ khui vbe ẹvbo.
entries.example_english: His farm is large in the land.

entries.example_edo: Ọmwan ne ọ ya ọ rre.
entries.example_english: The one who went has returned.

entries.example_edo: Ẹghẹ ne ọ ya ọ ye ẹnọ.
entries.example_english: The time that passed is today.

entries.example_edo: Ima gha sẹtin iran vbe isẹ.
entries.example_english: Let us help him in work.

entries.example_edo: Ọmwan hia gha rẹn ẹmwẹn Ẹdo.
entries.example_english: Everyone should know the Edo language.

entries.example_edo: Emwin ne ọ dẹ hia ọ rre vbe ẹvbo Ẹdo.
entries.example_english: All good things come from Edo land.

entries.example_edo: Ẹhẹ ne ima rẹn ọ khui.
Ẹvbo ne ima ya ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: The land we go to is good.

entries.example_edo: Ọ sẹtin ima vbe ẹghẹ hia.
entries.example_english: He helps us all the time.

entries.english: The king who rules the land
entries.example_edo: Ọba ne ọ rran ẹvbo ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: The king who rules the land is good.

entries.example_edo: Ọmwan ne ọ mwẹn ẹvbo ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: One who loves home is good.

entries.example_edo: Ima gha rẹn ẹmwẹn ima vbe iran.
entries.example_english: Let us know our language well.

entries.english: The matter is heavy on him
entries.example_edo: Emwin ọ khọ iran vbe ẹghẹ ẹre.
entries.example_english: The matter weighs on him in his time.

entries.example_edo: Ọ ya vbe ẹvbo ẹghẹ ne ọ rre.
entries.example_english: He traveled to his homeland recently.

entries.example_edo: Ẹtin ugbo ọ khui vbe ẹsuku.
entries.example_english: The bush tree is tall in the forest.

entries.example_edo: Ọ mudia ne ima vbe ẹghẹ isiẹ.
entries.example_english: He stood for us in difficult times.
The new land is good for him.
He sees us from the world.

entries.example_edo: Ima rọ ẹmwẹn ọmwan ne ọ ye.
entries.example_english: We listen to the elder's words.

entries.example_edo: Ọ kpẹ ogban vbe ẹghẹ hia.
entries.example_english: He carried bravery all the time.

entries.example_edo: Emwin ne ima rẹn ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: The things we know are good.

entries.example_edo: Ẹvbo Ẹdo ne iran ọ dẹ.

entries.english: The one who went to farm
entries.example_edo: Ọmwan ne ọ ya ẹzọ ọ rre.
entries.example_english: The one who went to farm has returned.

entries.example_edo: Ẹtin ne ọ da ọ khui.
Ọba ne ima rẹn ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: The king we know is good.

entries.example_edo: Ima sẹtin iran hia vbe ẹvbo.
entries.example_english: We help all of them in the land.

entries.edo: Ẹghẹ ne ọ rre vbe ẹnọ
entries.pronunciation: eh-gheh neh oh reh vbeh eh-noh
entries.example_edo: Ẹghẹ ne ọ rre vbe ẹnọ ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: The time that came today is good.

entries.example_edo: Ọ rhie orhe rẹn vbe ọsẹ.
entries.example_english: He gave me money at the market.

entries.example_edo: Ẹkhuẹ Ẹdo ne ima rẹn ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: The Edo dance we know is beautiful.

entries.example_edo: Ọ sẹe ima rẹn ẹmwẹn Ẹdo.
entries.example_english: He told us to know the Edo language.

entries.example_edo: Ivbi ne ima rhie ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: The children we raised are good.

entries.example_edo: Ọ ya ogiẹ vbe ẹvbo Ẹdo.
entries.example_english: He went to the Edo festival.

entries.edo: Orẹ ẹdo ne ima ya rẹn
entries.pronunciation: oh-reh eh-doh neh ee-mah yah rehn
entries.example_edo: Orẹ Ẹdo ne ima ya rẹn ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: The Edo love we seek is good.

entries.example_edo: Ighele Ẹdo ne ọ dẹ ọ ye ẹvbo.
entries.example_english: The beautiful Edo song is in the land.

entries.example_edo: Ẹmwẹn ẹrhẹn ọ ye ọmwan ne ọ ye.
entries.example_english: Words of wisdom come from the elder.

entries.english: We went to know his land
entries.example_edo: Ima ya rẹn iran ẹvbo Ẹdo.
entries.example_english: We went to know his Edo land.

entries.english: We know all of his things
entries.example_edo: Ima rẹn iran emwin hia ne ọ ye.
entries.example_english: We know all the things he has.

entries.example_edo: Ẹvbẹhẹ iran ọ yo vbe ẹghẹ.
entries.example_english: His heat has cooled with time.

entries.edo: Ọmwan ne ọ rẹn ẹmwẹn hia
entries.english: One who knows all the words
entries.pronunciation: ohm-wahn neh oh rehn ehm-wehn hee-ah
entries.example_edo: Ọmwan ne ọ rẹn ẹmwẹn hia ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: One who knows all the words is blessed.
The time of cleverness has come.

entries.english: Let us love all of them
entries.example_edo: Ima gha mwẹn iran hia vbe ẹvbo.
entries.example_english: Let us love all of them in the land.

entries.example_edo: Ọ da ẹmẹ agbon vbe ẹvbo.
entries.example_english: The river flowed through the land.

entries.example_edo: Ẹmwẹn Ẹdo ọ yo vbe agbon.
entries.example_english: The Edo language endures in the world.

entries.example_edo: Ẹdo ẹre ọ gha rẹn vbe ẹghẹ hia.
Ima mwẹn Ẹdo hia vbe ọnọ ima.
entries.example_english: We all love Edo in our hearts.

entries.example_edo: Ivbi Ẹdo gha rẹn ẹmwẹn ima.
entries.example_english: Children of Edo will know our language.

entries.english: Edo land will always be good
entries.example_edo: Ẹvbo Ẹdo ọ gha dẹ vbe ẹghẹ hia.
entries.example_english: Edo land will always be good.

entries.example_edo: Ọ rre ẹvbo Ẹdo, ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: He is from Edo land, he is fine.

entries.example_edo: Ẹmwẹn ima ọ gha ye vbe agbon.
entries.example_english: Our language will remain in the world.

entries.english: We will carry the Edo language
entries.example_edo: Ima gha kpẹ ẹmwẹn Ẹdo vbe ẹghẹ hia.
entries.example_english: We will carry the Edo language always.

entries.english: It is good on his part
entries.example_edo: Ọ dẹ vbe iran vbe ẹghẹ hia.
entries.example_english: It is always good on his part.
God will help all of us.

entries.english: Edo is what we seek to know
entries.example_edo: Ẹdo ẹre ima ya rẹn vbe ẹghẹ hia.
entries.example_english: Edo is what we always seek to know.

entries.edo: Ima gha sẹe iran ẹmwẹn ima
entries.english: We will tell them our language
entries.pronunciation: ee-mah ghah seh-eh ee-rahn ehm-wehn ee-mah
entries.example_edo: Ima gha sẹe iran ẹmwẹn ima ne ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: We will tell them our beautiful language.

entries.edo: BiZY ọ sẹtin ima rẹn Ẹdo
entries.pronunciation: beez-ee oh seh-tin ee-mah rehn eh-doh
entries.example_edo: BiZY ọ sẹtin ima rẹn Ẹdo vbe ẹghẹ hia.
entries.example_english: BiZY helps us learn Edo all the time.

proverbs.edo: Ọ gha kpolo ẹghẹ, ọ na gha kpolo ọghe.
proverbs.english: He who wastes time, wastes his own opportunity.

proverbs.edo: Ẹvbo ẹre ọ dẹ ne ọmwan ne ọ mwẹn iran.
proverbs.english: Home is sweet for the one who loves it.

proverbs.edo: Ọmwan hia rẹn emwin ẹre, ma ọmwan hia rẹn emwin ọmwan ọkpa.
proverbs.english: Everyone knows their own thing; not everyone knows another's.

proverbs.edo: Ẹtin ne ọ ye agbon ọ gha da vbe ẹghẹ.
proverbs.english: The tree that stands in the earth will fall in its time.

proverbs.edo: Ọsẹ ne ọ khui ọ gha ye emwin dẹ.
proverbs.english: A large market will always have good things.

proverbs.edo: Ima gha rẹn ẹmwẹn ima, ẹmwẹn ima ọ gha ye.
proverbs.english: If we know our language, our language will remain.

proverbs.edo: Ọmwan ne ọ ya ẹzọ ọ gha rẹn iran emwin dẹ.
proverbs.english: The one who goes to the farm will know good things.

proverbs.edo: Ẹrhẹn ọ ye ọmwan ne ọ rọ ẹmwẹn ọmwan ne ọ ye.
proverbs.english: Wisdom belongs to the one who listens to the elder.

proverbs.edo: Ọba ne ọ dẹ ọ gha sẹtin ẹvbo ẹre.
proverbs.english: A good king will always help his land.

proverbs.edo: Emwin ne ọ dẹ ọ rre vbe ẹghẹ ẹre.
proverbs.english: Good things come in their own time.

proverbs.edo: Ọmwan ne ọ mwẹn ẹvbo ẹre ọ gha rre ẹvbo.
proverbs.english: One who loves his homeland will always return home.

proverbs.edo: Ivbi ne ima rhie dẹ ima gha sẹtin ima rẹn.
proverbs.english: The children we raise well will help us to know.

proverbs.edo: Ẹdo ne ima rẹn ọ gha ye vbe agbon hia.
proverbs.english: The Edo we know will remain in all the world.

proverbs.edo: Ọmwan ne ọ gbe ẹrhẹn ọ gha rẹn ọkharhan dẹ.
proverbs.english: One who cultivates wisdom will know the right path.

proverbs.edo: Orẹ ẹvbo ọ khui ne orẹ agbon.
proverbs.english: Love of homeland is greater than love of the world.

proverbs.edo: Ẹmwẹn ne ọ dẹ ọ gha ye vbe ọnọ ọmwan ne ọ rọ iran.
proverbs.english: Words that are good will remain in the heart of the one who listens.

proverbs.edo: Ẹtin ne ọ khin ọ gha khui vbe ẹghẹ.
proverbs.english: A small tree will grow tall in time.

proverbs.edo: Ọmwan ne ọ kpẹ ogban ọ gha ya iran ẹvbo dẹ.
proverbs.english: One who carries bravery will reach his homeland safely.

proverbs.edo: Ima sẹtin ọmwan hia ne ima mwẹn.
proverbs.english: We help everyone that we love.

proverbs.edo: Ẹghẹ hia ọ dẹ ne ọmwan ne ọ rẹn iran Osanobua.
proverbs.english: All time is good for the one who knows God.

proverbs.edo: Ẹvbo Ẹdo ọ gha dẹ vbe ẹghẹ hia.
proverbs.english: Edo land will always be good.

proverbs.edo: Ọ ma ẹmwẹn ọmwan ne ọ ya ne ọ rre.
proverbs.english: Do not speak ill of the one who went and returned.

proverbs.edo: Ivbi Ẹdo gha rẹn, Ẹdo ọ gha dẹ.
proverbs.english: If the children of Edo learn, Edo will flourish.

proverbs.edo: Ọmwan ọkpa ma gha ya iran ọkharhan hia.
proverbs.english: One person cannot walk all the paths alone.

proverbs.edo: Ẹre ima gha sẹe iran: Ẹdo ọ dẹ!
proverbs.english: This is what we will say to them: Edo is good!

lessons.title: Basic Greetings
lessons.description: Learn to greet people in Edo at different times of day and in different situations.
objectives: Greet people correctly at morning, afternoon, and evening
Respond to greetings with appropriate phrases
Introduce yourself and ask for someone's name
Say goodbye in Edo

lessons.title: Numbers 1-20
lessons.description: Master counting in Edo from one to twenty, including basic number patterns.
objectives: Count from 1 to 10 in Edo
Count from 11 to 20 using Edo patterns
Understand the base-20 counting system
Use numbers in simple market transactions

lessons.title: Family Members
lessons.description: Learn the names for family members in Edo and how to talk about your family.
objectives: Name immediate family members in Edo
Say 'my mother', 'my father', 'my child'
Talk about your family unit
Understand possessive form with ẹre

lessons.title: Food & Eating
lessons.description: Discover traditional Edo foods and learn phrases for eating, hunger, and meals.
objectives: Name 8 traditional Edo foods
Express hunger and satisfaction in Edo
Ask for food and water politely
Understand the cultural importance of communal eating

lessons.title: Basic Actions & Verbs
lessons.description: Learn the most common verbs used in everyday Edo conversation.
objectives: Use 9 essential Edo verbs correctly
Form simple present-tense sentences
Describe daily activities in Edo
Understand verb placement in Edo sentences

lessons.title: Body Parts & Health
lessons.description: Learn body part vocabulary and how to describe pain and illness in Edo.
objectives: Name 9 major body parts in Edo
Describe location of pain using khian
Ask for medical help in Edo
Understand how to indicate possession with body parts

lessons.title: Time & Days
lessons.description: Master time expressions, days, and telling time in the Edo language.
objectives: Express past, present, and future time
Name time periods: morning, afternoon, evening, night
Talk about days, weeks, months, and years
Form temporal sentences in Edo

lessons.title: Places & Directions
lessons.description: Navigate Edo-speaking areas by learning place names and direction-giving vocabulary.
objectives: Name 6 important places in Edo
Give and receive directions in Edo
Ask where things are located
Understand spatial prepositions in Edo

lessons.title: Emotions & Feelings
lessons.description: Express your feelings and emotions authentically in the Edo language.
objectives: Express happiness, sadness, anger, and fear in Edo
Describe emotional states using compound phrases
Ask someone how they feel emotionally
Understand cultural norms around expressing emotion

lessons.title: Colors & Descriptions
lessons.description: Describe objects using Edo color words and adjectives.
objectives: Name 5 basic colors in Edo
Use adjectives to describe size and age
Form descriptive sentences with Edo adjectives
Understand adjective placement in Edo

lessons.title: Edo Culture & Tradition
lessons.description: Explore the rich cultural vocabulary of the Edo people — royalty, festivals, and traditions.
objectives: Understand key cultural and royal titles in Edo
Describe traditional festivals and ceremonies
Talk about Edo music, dance, and art
Appreciate the role of spirituality in Edo culture

lessons.title: Proverbs & Wisdom
lessons.description: Study traditional Edo proverbs and the deep wisdom they contain.
objectives: Memorize 5 traditional Edo proverbs
Understand the social context of Edo proverbs
Use proverbs appropriately in conversation
Recognize the moral teachings in Edo oral tradition

lessons.title: Market & Commerce
lessons.description: Confidently navigate Edo markets and conduct business in the Edo language.
objectives: Negotiate prices in Edo
Ask about goods and quantities
Complete a transaction entirely in Edo
Understand market day customs

lessons.title: Religion & Spirituality
lessons.description: Learn vocabulary for spiritual and religious expression in the Edo tradition.
objectives: Understand key spiritual terms in Edo
Express blessings and prayers in Edo
Know the significance of Osanobua in Edo belief
Recognize sacred vocabulary in Edo contexts

lessons.title: Advanced Conversation
lessons.description: Put it all together with complex sentences, storytelling, and full conversations in Edo.
objectives: Hold a 5-minute conversation entirely in Edo
Tell a simple story using Edo grammar
Use complex sentences with multiple clauses
Understand spoken Edo at natural speed
